name: Do-Stuff-Helper
description: A Claude Code plugin for guided execution of personal projects and life goals.

phases.title: Complete the Core Pipeline
phases.goal: Get the full discover → roadmap → waypoint design → decompose → execute loop working end-to-end.

phases.title: Feedback & Replanning
phases.goal: Close the learning loops so the system improves itself and adapts to change.

phases.title: Monitoring & Dashboard
phases.goal: Unified visibility across all activities with independent analysis agents.

phases.title: Life Coaching & Advisory
phases.goal: Advisory agents that help the user stay aligned with their values and life goals.

waypoints.title: Roadmap Skill
waypoints.why: Every future activity needs this. We're bootstrapping manually right now, which doesn't scale.
waypoints.done_when: A roadmap skill exists that reads a brief, interviews the user about priorities and sequencing, and produces an adaptive waypoint-based roadmap as JSON. Includes suggest-and-confirm transition from discover.

waypoints.title: Waypoint Storage Format
waypoints.why: We need to decide how waypoints are stored before other skills can read/write them. Affects every downstream skill.
waypoints.done_when: A storage format is defined, documented in CLAUDE.md, and the roadmap skill uses it.

waypoints.title: Waypoint Design Skill
waypoints.why: Waypoints need sufficient descriptions before they can be decomposed. This is the bridge between high-level planning and task execution.
waypoints.done_when: A skill exists that evaluates a waypoint, scales its design effort to complexity, and produces a description sufficient for confident decomposition. Simple waypoints get a sentence; complex ones get detailed designs or decision docs.

waypoints.title: Decompose & Execute Architecture
waypoints.why: This is the core engine — recursive task decomposition and parallel worker execution.
waypoints.done_when: A manager agent can take a designed waypoint, decompose it into parallelizable tasks, and orchestrate worker agents to execute them. Workers operate in their own context and report status back.

waypoints.title: Autonomy Model
waypoints.why: Workers need to know what they're allowed to do without asking.
waypoints.done_when: Permission tier system is implemented — built-ins and installed skills/MCP servers allowed by default, ask-and-learn for everything else with manual/once/always/never options. New 'always' approvals create skills automatically.

waypoints.title: Pilot Activity — End-to-End
waypoints.why: Validates the entire pipeline on a real activity before broadening.
waypoints.done_when: One pilot activity (wildlife cards or personal branding) has been run through discover → roadmap → waypoint design → decompose → execute, with at least one waypoint fully completed by workers.

waypoints.title: Backlog & Replanning Process
waypoints.why: The roadmap needs to adapt as we learn. New insights, scope changes, and discovered work need to be incorporated.
waypoints.done_when: An autonomous process captures updates (new waypoints, scope changes, reprioritizations) and periodically incorporates them into the roadmap. User is notified of significant changes.

waypoints.title: Dashboard — Core
waypoints.why: As multiple activities run in parallel, the user needs a single place to see everything.
waypoints.done_when: A dashboard shows all activities with: current status, recent changelog, blockers assigned to user, next actions, and whether a Claude instance is actively working.

waypoints.title: Inbox
waypoints.why: Workers need a way to ask questions async without blocking.
waypoints.done_when: Workers can send questions to a message queue. User can view and reply at their convenience. Worker picks up the response and continues.

waypoints.title: Forward Motion Analyst
waypoints.why: Keeps the user focused on what matters most each week.
waypoints.done_when: A weekly agent reviews progress across activities, assesses alignment with goals, produces a week-ahead plan with 2-3 priorities, and refreshes as priorities are completed.

waypoints.title: Work Checker
waypoints.why: Quality gate for autonomous worker output.
waypoints.done_when: An agent reviews worker output for quality, either at the manager level or as a standalone reviewer. Flags low-quality work before it's committed.

waypoints.title: Priority Alignment & Blocker Auto-Resolution
waypoints.why: Surfaces misalignment between effort and stated priorities; auto-resolves blockers where possible.
waypoints.done_when: Agents periodically check effort distribution, surface observations on dashboard, and attempt to resolve blockers autonomously before escalating.

waypoints.title: User Profile Builder
waypoints.why: All advisory agents need a profile of the user's personality, habits, and goals.
waypoints.done_when: An interview-based skill builds a user profile covering personality, work styles, habits, and life goals. Profile is stored and accessible to other agents.

waypoints.title: Advisory Agent Pattern
waypoints.why: Multiple coach types share the same shape — design it once.
waypoints.done_when: A reusable pattern exists for advisory agents: maintain user profile access, observe across activities, initiate conversations, advisory-only. At least one concrete agent (life coach) built on the pattern.

waypoints.title: Chat Integration & Notifications
waypoints.why: Advisory agents need a channel to engage in dialog with the user.
waypoints.done_when: Advisory agents can send messages and receive replies via a chat platform (likely Telegram). Monitoring notifications also route through this channel.

waypoints.title: Home Server Browser Automation Setup
waypoints.why: Execution agents need browser capability for web tasks, authenticated site interaction, and data extraction. Claude in Chrome integration enables human intervention for CAPTCHAs and login flows.
waypoints.done_when: Xvfb + VNC + Chrome + Claude in Chrome extension are installed and working on the N100. Claude Code can control Chrome via --chrome flag. User can VNC in to observe or intervene. Setup survives reboot.

waypoints.title: Fix Discover Skill Invocation Bug
waypoints.why: The do-stuff-helper:discover skill returned a disable-model-invocation error when invoked via the Skill tool, despite not having that flag set.
waypoints.done_when: Root cause identified and fixed, or workaround documented.

waypoints.title: Plugin Update Workflow
waypoints.why: Updating the plugin currently requires manually pulling the marketplace clone. This friction slows development.
waypoints.done_when: A documented (or automated) workflow exists for: bump version → commit → push → pull marketplace clone → reinstall plugin.

waypoints.title: Suggest-and-Confirm Transition in Discover
waypoints.why: Discover should prompt the user to continue to roadmap planning when it finishes.
waypoints.done_when: Discover skill's Step 11 includes a suggest-and-confirm prompt to invoke the roadmap skill.

waypoints.title: Mine CLAUDE.md for Activity Template Instructions
waypoints.why: do-stuff-helper's CLAUDE.md contains instructions (how to use skills, conventions, etc.) that should be seeded into every new activity's CLAUDE.md so agents working in those repos know how to use the system.
waypoints.done_when: The organize skill (or a post-organize step) seeds new activity CLAUDE.md files with relevant do-stuff-helper instructions extracted from the plugin's own CLAUDE.md.

waypoints.title: Evaluate T-Shirt Sizing for Waypoints
waypoints.why: After running the pipeline on real activities, evaluate whether adding size labels (S/M/L/XL) to waypoints helps with prioritization, effort estimation, or planning.
waypoints.done_when: A decision is made on whether t-shirt sizing adds value, and if so, the roadmap schema and roadmap skill are updated to support it.

waypoints.title: Brief-Roadmap Synchronization
waypoints.why: Skills that modify the roadmap don't update the brief, causing drift between what the activity is about and what it's actually doing.
waypoints.done_when: All skills that add or modify waypoints (roadmap, replan, waypoint-design) also update the relevant sections of the brief. Brief stays current as the source of truth for activity scope.

waypoints.title: Build Rhythm Capability
waypoints.why: Activities produce recurring progressive schedules (training plans, writing habits) that don't fit the one-time task/waypoint model.
waypoints.done_when: A rhythm artifact type exists that captures recurring behavioral patterns. Rhythms integrate with the roadmap (waypoint produces a rhythm), dashboard (show rhythm status), and can be exported to calendar tools.

waypoints.title: Add Replan Reminder via n8n
waypoints.why: Backlog review depends on the user remembering to run it. A scheduled trigger keeps the roadmap fresh automatically.
waypoints.done_when: An n8n workflow triggers the replan skill on a recurring schedule (e.g., weekly) via the Claude webhook.

waypoints.title: Auto-Dispatch Ready Work
waypoints.why: Remove human bottleneck from starting planned work. Users should only intervene for decisions, not to say 'go'.
waypoints.done_when: An n8n workflow or webhook hook detects waypoints in planned status with unblocked dependencies and triggers waypoint-implement automatically.

waypoints.title: Implement Skill Bug Fixes
waypoints.why: Known bugs: human tasks not written to inbox.json, session-restart tasks not inserted in dependency graph.
waypoints.done_when: Both bugs verified fixed: human tasks appear in inbox.json when surfaced, and session-restart dependencies are inserted when skills change mid-implementation.

waypoints.title: Worker Verification Step
waypoints.why: Workers don't systematically verify output against acceptance criteria before marking tasks complete.
waypoints.done_when: The implement skill includes a verification step where workers validate their output matches acceptance criteria before marking tasks complete.

waypoints.title: Front-End Design Plugin in Organize
waypoints.why: Activities involving UI work lack design system guidance out of the box.
waypoints.done_when: The organize skill installs a front-end/UI design plugin when bootstrapping activities that involve building user interfaces.
